steps.title: Simplify the left side
steps.content: $\frac{14x}{7y} = \frac{2x}{y}$, so the equation becomes:$$\frac{2x}{y} = 2\sqrt{w+19}$$Divide both sides by 2:$$\frac{x}{y} = \sqrt{w+19}$$

steps.title: Eliminate the square root by squaring both sides
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $w = \sqrt{\frac{x}{y}} - 19$: takes the square root instead of squaring — reverses the operation. Incorrect.
  • B) $w = \sqrt{\frac{28x}{14y}} - 19$: fails to simplify $\frac{14x}{7y}$ correctly and takes a square root instead of squaring. Incorrect.
  • C) $w = \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2 - 19$: matches the derived result exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D) $w = \left(\frac{28x}{14y}\right)^2 - 19$: simplifies $\frac{28x}{14y} = \frac{2x}{y}$, but forgets to divide by 2 before squaring, so $\left(\frac{2x}{y}\right)^2 = \frac{4x^2}{y^2} \neq \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2$. Incorrect.
